 {"id":"bd-411u","title":"Document BEADS_DIR pattern for multi-agent workspaces (Gas Town)","description":"Gas Town and similar multi-agent systems need to configure separate beads databases per workspace/rig, distinct from any project-level beads.

## Use Case

In Gas Town:
- Each 'rig' (managed project) has multiple agents (polecats, refinery, witness)
- All agents in a rig should share a single beads database at the rig level
- This should be separate from any .beads/ the project itself uses
- The BEADS_DIR env var enables this

## Documentation Needed

1. Add a section to docs explaining BEADS_DIR for multi-agent setups
2. Example: setting BEADS_DIR in agent startup scripts/hooks
3. Clarify interaction with project-level .beads/ (BEADS_DIR takes precedence)

## Current Support

Already implemented in internal/beads/beads.go:FindDatabasePath():
- BEADS_DIR env var is checked first (preferred)
- BEADS_DB env var still supported (deprecated)
- Falls back to .beads/ search in tree

Just needs documentation for the multi-agent workspace pattern.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T22:08:22.158027-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T22:08:22.158027-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-47tn","title":"Add bd daemon --stop-all command to kill all daemon processes","description":"Currently there's no easy way to stop all running bd daemon processes. Users must resort to pkill -f 'bd daemon' or similar shell commands.

Add a --stop-all flag to bd daemon that:
1. Finds all running bd daemon processes (not just the current repo's daemon)
2. Gracefully stops them all
3. Reports how many were stopped

This is useful when:
- Multiple daemons are running and causing race conditions
- User wants a clean slate before running bd sync
- Debugging daemon-related issues","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-13T06:34:45.080633-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T01:14:49.501989-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-14T17:33:03.057089-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-49kw","title":"Workaround for FastMCP outputSchema bug in Claude Code","description":"The beads MCP server (v0.23.1) successfully connects to Claude Code, but all tools fail to load with a schema validation error due to a bug in FastMCP 2.13.1.

Error: "Invalid literal value, expected \"object\"" in outputSchema.

Root Cause: FastMCP generates outputSchema with $ref at root level without "type": "object" for self-referential models (Issue).

Workaround: Use slash commands (/beads:ready) or wait for FastMCP fix.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-20T18:55:39.041831-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-20T18:55:39.041831-05:00"}
+{"id":"bd-49kw","title":"Workaround for FastMCP outputSchema bug in Claude Code","description":"The beads MCP server (v0.23.1) successfully connects to Claude Code, but all tools fail to load with a schema validation error due to a bug in FastMCP 2.13.1.

Error: "Invalid literal value, expected \"object\"" in outputSchema.

Root Cause: FastMCP generates outputSchema with $ref at root level without "type": "object" for self-referential models (Issue).

Workaround: Use slash commands (/beads:ready) or wait for FastMCP fix.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-20T18:55:39.041831-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:43:14.088318-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:43:14.088318-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in v0.24.0+ (commit f3a678f) - see integrations/beads-mcp/README.md line 231-249"}
 {"id":"bd-4ec8","title":"Widespread double JSON encoding bug in daemon mode RPC calls","description":"Multiple CLI commands had the same double JSON encoding bug found in bd-1048. All commands that called ResolveID via RPC used string(resp.Data) instead of properly unmarshaling the JSON response. This caused IDs to retain JSON quotes ("bd-1048" instead of bd-1048), which then got double-encoded when passed to subsequent RPC calls.

Affected commands:
- bd show (3 instances)
- bd dep add/remove/tree (5 instances)
- bd label add/remove/list (3 instances)
- bd reopen (1 instance)

Root cause: resp.Data is json.RawMessage (already JSON-encoded), so string() conversion preserves quotes.

Fix: Replace all string(resp.Data) with json.Unmarshal(resp.Data, &id) for proper deserialization.

All commands now tested and working correctly with daemon mode.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-02T22:33:01.632691-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:26:05.851197-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T16:26:05.851197-08:00","close_reason":"Fix verified: all ResolveID responses now use json.Unmarshal instead of string(resp.Data)"}

 {"id":"bd-au0","title":"Command Set Standardization & Flag Consistency","description":"Comprehensive improvements to bd command set based on 2025 audit findings.

## Background
See docs/command-audit-2025.md for detailed analysis.

## Goals
1. Standardize flag naming and behavior across all commands
2. Add missing flags for feature parity
3. Fix naming confusion
4. Improve consistency in JSON output

## Success Criteria
- All mutating commands support --dry-run (no --preview variants)
- bd update supports label operations
- bd search has filter parity with bd list
- Priority flags accept both int and P0-P4 format everywhere
- JSON output is consistent across all commands","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-21T21:05:55.672749-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-21T21:05:55.672749-05:00"}
 {"id":"bd-au0.10","title":"Add global verbosity flags (--verbose, --quiet)","description":"Add consistent verbosity controls across all commands.

**Current state:**
- bd init has --quiet flag
- No other commands have verbosity controls
- Debug output controlled by BD_VERBOSE env var

**Proposal:**
Add persistent flags:
- --verbose / -v: Enable debug output
- --quiet / -q: Suppress non-essential output

**Implementation:**
- Add to rootCmd.PersistentFlags()
- Replace BD_VERBOSE checks with flag checks
- Standardize output levels:
  * Quiet: Errors only
  * Normal: Errors + success messages
  * Verbose: Errors + success + debug info

**Files to modify:**
- cmd/bd/main.go (add flags)
- internal/debug/debug.go (respect flags)
- Update all commands to respect quiet mode

**Testing:**
- Verify --verbose shows debug output
- Verify --quiet suppresses normal output
- Ensure errors always show regardless of mode","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-21T21:08:21.600209-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-21T21:08:21.600209-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-au0.10","depends_on_id":"bd-au0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-21T21:08:21.602557-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-au0.7","title":"Audit and standardize JSON output across all commands","description":"Ensure consistent JSON format and error handling when --json flag is used.

**Scope:**
1. Verify all commands respect --json flag
2. Standardize success response format
3. Standardize error response format
4. Document JSON schemas

**Commands to audit:**
- Core CRUD: create, update, delete, show, list, search ✓
- Queries: ready, blocked, stale, count, stats, status
- Deps: dep add/remove/tree/cycles
- Labels: label commands
- Comments: comments add/list/delete
- Epics: epic status/close-eligible
- Export/import: already support --json ✓

**Testing:**
- Success cases return valid JSON
- Error cases return valid JSON (not plain text)
- Consistent field naming (snake_case vs camelCase)
- Array vs object wrapping consistency","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-21T21:07:35.304424-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-21T21:07:35.304424-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-au0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-au0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-21T21:07:35.305663-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-au0.8","title":"Improve clean vs cleanup command naming/documentation","description":"Clarify the difference between bd clean and bd cleanup to reduce user confusion.

**Current state:**
- bd clean: Remove temporary artifacts (.beads/bd.sock, logs, etc.)
- bd cleanup: Delete old closed issues from database

**Options:**
1. Rename for clarity:
   - bd clean → bd clean-temp
   - bd cleanup → bd cleanup-issues
   
2. Keep names but improve help text and documentation

3. Add prominent warnings in help output

**Preferred approach:** Option 2 (improve documentation)
- Update short/long descriptions in commands
- Add examples to help text
- Update README.md
- Add cross-references in help output

**Files to modify:**
- cmd/bd/clean.go
- cmd/bd/cleanup.go
- README.md or ADVANCED.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-21T21:07:49.960534-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-21T21:07:49.960534-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-au0.8","depends_on_id":"bd-au0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-21T21:07:49.962743-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-au0.9","title":"Review and document rarely-used commands","description":"Document use cases or consider deprecation for infrequently-used commands.

**Commands to review:**
1. bd rename-prefix - How often is this used? Document use cases
2. bd detect-pollution - Consider integrating into bd validate
3. bd migrate-hash-ids - One-time migration, keep but document as legacy

**For each command:**
- Document typical use cases
- Add examples to help text
- Consider if it should be a subcommand instead
- Add deprecation warning if appropriate

**Not changing:**
- duplicates ✓ (useful for data quality)
- repair-deps ✓ (useful for fixing broken refs)
- restore ✓ (critical for compacted issues)
- compact ✓ (performance feature)

**Deliverable:**
- Updated help text
- Documentation in ADVANCED.md
- Deprecation plan if needed","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-21T21:08:05.588275-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-21T21:08:05.588275-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-au0.9","depends_on_id":"bd-au0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-21T21:08:05.59003-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- cmd/bd/doctor.go - checkDeletionsManifest
- Plus many more

Action: Aggressively remove all non-migration deletions code.  {"id":"bd-fu83","title":"Fix daemon/direct mode inconsistency in relate and duplicate commands","description":"The relate.go and duplicate.go commands have inconsistent daemon/direct mode handling:

When daemonClient is connected, they resolve IDs via RPC but then perform updates directly via store.UpdateIssue(), bypassing the daemon.

Affected locations:
- relate.go:125-139 (runRelate update)
- relate.go:235-246 (runUnrelate update)  
- duplicate.go:120 (runDuplicate update)
- duplicate.go:207 (runSupersede update)

Should either use RPC for updates when daemon is running, or document why direct access is intentional.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T20:52:54.164189-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:34:20.861751-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:34:20.861751-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 3ec517cc - commands now properly use RPC when daemon is connected"}

 {"id":"bd-tbz3","title":"bd init UX Improvements","description":"bd init leaves users with incomplete setup, requiring manual bd doctor --fix.
